resources.string: Waiting for card to write key...
Ready to overwrite data on NFC card.
Key File not available. Please scan NFC first.
Key not available. Please tap NFC card to reload.

Generate a new secure 128-byte key.

Please check your NFC card capacity before generating.
Different NFC cards have different storage sizes.
NFC capacity must be considered before storing the key file.
KeePass NFC Bridge is an open source software for interacting with NFC Hardware vaults.

Key File Imported
Size: %1$d Bytes
SHA-256: %2$s

Tap NFC Card to Save.
Hold NFC card to format entirely.

Generate a new secure 128-byte key.

Please check your NFC card capacity before generating.
Different NFC cards have different storage sizes.
NFC capacity must be considered before storing the key file.

Key File Generated
Size: %1$d Bytes
SHA-256: %2$s

Tap NFC Card to Save.
KeePassDX not found. Please install KeePassDX first.
Waiting for NFC Card...\n\nPlease tap your NFC card now to erase the Key File.
Force Overwrite Mode Active.\n\nTap NFC card now!

Loaded From NFC / RAM Active
Size: %1$d Bytes
SHA-256:
%2$s
Session Expired! No key to export.
Key File not available in RAM

KeePass NFC Bridge is an open-source Android security application created to serve as a bridge between KeePassDX and NFC hardware.

This application is designed to help users manage their KeePass Key File more securely using NFC technology.

KeePass NFC Bridge is not a replacement password manager.

This application does not replace KeePassDX.

KeePass NFC Bridge works as a:
Hardware Security Bridge

connecting:
KeePassDX
with
NFC Hardware Storage

The main goal of this application is to provide a safer method of storing and using Key Files without having to permanently store sensitive files in the device's internal storage.

KeePass is a password manager system that uses an encrypted database.

In KeePass usage, there are usually two security components:

1. KeePass Database
Format: .kdbx
Contains: Passwords, Usernames, Account info, User secrets.

2. Key File
Format: keyfile.bin
Used as an additional factor to unlock the database.

Common problem:
The KeePass Database and Key File are often stored on the same device.
If the device is compromised, both security components can be exposed.

KeePass NFC Bridge was created to separate these two components.

Concept:
Database: Still managed by KeePassDX.
Key File: Stored on an NFC card.

With this method, the user has an additional hardware-based security layer.

KeePass NFC Bridge is used for:

✓ Generating KeePass Key Files.
✓ Importing the user's existing Key File.
✓ Writing a Key File to an NFC card.
✓ Reading a Key File via NFC.
✓ Verifying data integrity using SHA-256.
✓ Providing a temporary Key File for KeePassDX.
✓ Wiping sensitive data from RAM after the session ends.

This application is built for users who want to enhance KeePass security with a method that is:
Offline.
Cloudless.
Hardware-based.
Fully user-controlled.

----------------------------------
STAGE 1
Generate or Import Key File
----------------------------------
Users can:
- Create a new Key File.
- Use an existing Key File.

During the process, the application performs:
- File validation.
- File size checking.
- Checksum creation.
- Data preparation for NFC.

----------------------------------
STAGE 2
Writing Key File to NFC
----------------------------------
The Key File is not written as plain data.
KeePass NFC Bridge uses a special container:
KPNFC01 Container

The container stores:
- Identifier.
- Version.
- Key File size.
- Metadata.
- Key File data.
- Verification info.

Before writing:
The application checks:
- NFC card type.
- Card capacity.
- NFC structure.
- Data to be written.

----------------------------------
STAGE 3
Reading NFC Card
----------------------------------
When the user taps the NFC card:
The application performs:
- Card type detection.
- NFC technology reading.
- Searching for KPNFC01 Container.
- Reading Key File data.
- Validation.

----------------------------------
STAGE 4
Security Verification
----------------------------------
Before the Key File is used:
The application performs:
- Integrity check.
- SHA-256 verification.
- Ensuring data has not changed.

If valid:
Key File can be used.

If invalid:
Data is rejected.

----------------------------------
STAGE 5
Temporary RAM Processing
----------------------------------
After success:
The Key File is loaded temporarily into RAM.

Key File:
Is not stored permanently.
Is not backed up automatically.
Is not sent outside the device.

When the session ends:
- RAM is cleared.
- Sensitive data is deleted.
- Session becomes inactive.

KeePass NFC Bridge uses a hardware-based security concept.

The main goal of this system is to separate security factors so that access does not rely on just one component.

The security system consists of two factors:

----------------------------------
1. Knowledge Factor
----------------------------------
KeePass master password.
The master password is known only to the user and is used by KeePassDX to open the KeePass database.
KeePass NFC Bridge does not know the user's master password.

----------------------------------
2. Possession Factor
----------------------------------
The NFC card storing the Key File.
The NFC card becomes the physical medium the user must possess to provide the Key File.

Without the NFC card:
The user does not have the Key File.

Without the master password:
The user cannot open the KeePass database.

With the combination of these two factors, the user gains an additional hardware-based security layer.
---------------------------------- NFC Security Differences ---------------------------------- Standard NFC tags can be used for basic key file storage.
Secure NFC products provide additional hardware-based security features depending on the card capability.
For password manager security usage, NTAG424 DNA or higher security NFC hardware is recommended.

KeePass NFC Bridge is built on the principle:
Users have full control over their data.

This application does not send user data to servers or third parties.
All processes are performed locally on the user's device.

KeePass NFC Bridge does not know:
- The user's KeePass master password.
- The contents of the KeePass database (.kdbx).
- User account data.
- The user's Key File after the session ends.

The application developer has no access to the user's personal data.

The application does not collect:
- Passwords.
- Key Files.
- KeePass Databases.
- Personal information.
- Data from the user's NFC card.

The application's only functions are:
- Reading NFC cards.
- Validating data.
- Processing the Key File temporarily.
- Providing the Key File for KeePassDX.
- Managing the RAM session.

There is no:
- Cloud synchronization.
- Automatic data transmission.
- User data collection.
- User activity tracking.

All sensitive data remains on the user's device.

KeePass NFC Bridge is designed to work offline.
The application does not require an internet connection to perform its main functions.

All main processes are performed locally on the user's device, including:
- Generate Key File.
- Import Key File.
- Reading NFC cards.
- Writing data to NFC cards.
- Data integrity verification.
- Temporary Key File processing in RAM.
- Shared usage with KeePassDX.

KeePass NFC Bridge does not use external servers and does not send the user's sensitive data to the internet.

The internet is not needed for daily use.

The user retains full control over:
- Key Files.
- NFC Cards.
- KeePass Databases.
- Personal security data.

KeePass NFC Bridge is designed to work with various NFC technologies.
The application will check the card capabilities before performing read or write operations.

Supported NFC types:

--------------------------------
NTAG Series
--------------------------------
✓ NTAG213
✓ NTAG215
✓ NTAG216

Used for:
- NFC data storage.
- KPNFC01 container.
- Key File storage.

--------------------------------
NTAG DNA Series
--------------------------------
✓ NTAG413 DNA
✓ NTAG424 DNA
✓ NTAG424 DNA TagTamper

Supports additional security features:
- AES-128 cryptography.
- Secure Unique NFC Message (SUN).
- Protection against cloning.
- Hardware chip-based authentication.
- Security features depend on chip capabilities.

--------------------------------
NTAG 22x DNA Series
--------------------------------
✓ NTAG223 DNA
✓ NTAG224 DNA

Used for authentication needs and NFC applications with additional security.

--------------------------------
MIFARE Family
--------------------------------
✓ MIFARE Ultralight
✓ MIFARE Classic
✓ MIFARE DESFire

Support depends on hardware capabilities and card implementation.

--------------------------------
NFC Standards
--------------------------------
✓ ISO/IEC 14443 Type A
✓ ISO/IEC 14443 Type B
✓ ISO/IEC 15693

Note:
Not all NFC cards have the same capabilities.
Storage capacity, security level, protocol, and hardware features depend on the type of card used.
The application will check the card capabilities before performing NFC operations.

KeePass NFC Bridge is open-source software.

License:
GNU General Public License v3.0
(GPL-3.0)

This project was created openly with the goals of:
- Providing code transparency.
- Allowing security to be audited.
- Supporting community contributions.

Users can study, use, and contribute to the project in accordance with the license rules.

Developer:
Miftahdhe

GitHub:
https://github.com/miftahdhe

----------------------------------
Open Source Support
----------------------------------
If KeePass NFC Bridge is useful to you, you can support the project's development through cryptocurrency donations.

Donations help with:
- Development of new features.
- Application testing.
- Project maintenance.
- Application security development.
